title: Weather & Conditions
description: Driving in rain, fog, snow, at night, and across railroad tracks.

scenarios.prompt: The road is wet from rain. What should you do?

choices.label: Slow down and keep more distance
Drive the same as on a dry day
Brake hard often to test the road

feedback.correct: Yes — wet roads are slippery. Slow down and leave extra space to stop.
feedback.same: Wet roads need extra care. Slow down and keep more distance. Try again.
feedback.fast: Going fast on wet roads can make you slide. Slow down. Try again.
feedback.brake: Hard braking on a wet road can make you skid. Just slow down gently. Try again.
feedback.bright: High beams don't help in rain. Slow down and keep more distance. Try again.
feedback.default: Slow down and keep extra distance on wet roads. Try again.

scenarios.prompt: It's foggy and hard to see. Which lights should you use?

feedback.correct: Yes — use low beams in fog. High beams bounce off the fog and make it harder to see.
feedback.high: High beams reflect off fog and blind you. Use low beams. Try again.
feedback.none: You still need lights in fog — use low beams. Try again.
feedback.hazard: Don't drive with hazard lights on — they're for a stopped car. Use low beams. Try again.
feedback.default: Use low beams in fog. Try again.

scenarios.prompt: The railroad lights are flashing and the gate is coming down. What should you do?

choices.label: Stop and wait for the train to pass
Drive across quickly before the train

feedback.correct: Yes — stop and wait. Never try to beat a train or go around the gate.
feedback.race: Never try to beat a train — stop and wait. Try again.
feedback.around: Never drive around a lowered gate. Stop and wait for the train. Try again.
feedback.creep: Don't move onto the tracks at all — stop and wait for the train. Try again.
feedback.default: Stop and wait for the train. Try again.
